title: Quality Commission Directive
content:
Quality Commission Directive

ISTANBUL ESENYURT UNIVERSITY

CHAPTER ONE

Purpose, Scope, Basis and Definitions

Purpose

(1) the aim of this directive Istanbul Esenyurt University of education, research and social contribution activities and administrative services for the implementation of quality assurance processes, in order to ensure continuity and sustainability in the work of the Commission defines the rules and procedures established quality.

Scope

(1) This directive covers the provisions related to the establishment and work of the Quality Commission of Istanbul Esenyurt University.

Basis

(1) This directive has been prepared in accordance with Articles 16 and 17 of the Higher Education Quality Assurance and Higher Education Quality Board Regulation published in the Official Gazette dated November 23, 2018 and numbered 30604 and the definitions contained in the Regulation.

Definitions

(1) What is stated in this directive;

a) Accreditation: Accreditation of academic and field-specific standards predetermined by an external evaluator institution in a particular field

the evaluation and external quality assurance process that measures whether it is met by a higher education program,

b) External Evaluator: Appointed by the Higher Education Quality Board to serve in the institutional external evaluation program.

the person who is competent to carry out the assigned external evaluation process,

c) External Evaluation: Independent external evaluation and accreditation of the quality of a higher education institution or program

the process of evaluation by organizations,

d) Internal Evaluation: To evaluate the quality of a higher education institution or program in itself,

e) Quality Assessment Registration Certificate: of independent institutions or organizations approved by the Higher Education Quality Board; certain

the academic and field-specific standards determined in a field are met by a higher education program and

a document indicating that it is authorized to evaluate the external evaluation and quality assurance process that measures that it has not been met,

f) Quality Commission: Higher Education within the scope of the Higher Education Quality Assurance and Higher Education Quality Board Regulation

Organization of quality assessment and assurance studies and accreditation studies established within the body of the Institution and

the commission responsible for the execution of,

g) Internal Evaluation Report of the Institution (KIDR): Education-training, research and social contribution activities of the higher education institution and

the report prepared every year by the higher education institution in order to monitor the quality assurance processes related to administrative services,

h) Institutional External Evaluation Program: Education-training, research and social contribution activities of higher education institutions with

the quality of administrative services is periodically determined by the Higher Education Quality Board at least once every five years.

the external evaluation process to be carried out,

i) Institutional Feedback Report (KGBR): Higher education evaluated within the scope of the institutional external evaluation program

a report prepared by external evaluators for the institution containing the strong and improvement-prone areas of the institution,

j) Performance Indicators: To measure whether or not the higher education institutions have achieved their goals and objectives or how much they have achieved,

tools used to monitor and evaluate,

k) Program Evaluation: Evaluation of teaching programs as part of the internal quality assurance system in higher education institutions

evaluation of their work,

l) Program Accreditation: Pre-determined in a certain field by independent external evaluation and accreditation organizations

external evaluation that measures whether academic and field-specific standards are met by a higher education program

and the quality assurance process,

m) Rector: The Rector of Istanbul Esenyurt University,

n) Senate: The Senate of Istanbul Esenyurt University,

o) Turkish Qualifications Framework: Designed to be compatible with the European Qualifications Framework; primary, secondary and higher education

including, vocational, general and academic education and training programs and all the qualification principles gained through other ways of learning

showing the national qualifications framework,

p) Higher Education Quality Assurance Status Report: Higher education that has been evaluated externally by the Board during the relevant year

taking into account the institutional evaluation reports of the institutions, prepared every year and primarily by the Council of Higher Education

the report presented to the information of the relevant stakeholders,

q) University: Istanbul Esenyurt University,

r) Higher Education Quality Board: Established by the Higher Education Quality Assurance and Higher Education Quality Board Regulation,

organization of quality assessment and assurance studies and accreditation studies in higher education institutions and

the Board responsible for the execution of,

s) Higher Education Quality Assurance System: Education-training, research and social contribution activities of higher education institutions and

internal and external quality assurance of administrative services, accreditation processes and independent external evaluation and accreditation organizations

principles related to the recognition and authorization processes,

expresses.

THE SECOND CHAPTER

Formation of the Quality Commission, Working Procedures and Principles, Duties

Formation of the Quality Commission

(1) The Rector is the chairman of the Commission, and the Vice-Rector is the Vice-Rector when the Rector is not present.

(2) The members of the commission shall consist of the members determined by the senate, representing the same faculty, institute, college, vocational school, not more than one and being from different fields of science. The members of the commission also include the Secretary General of the higher education institution, the Student Representative and the Director of the Professional Qualification and Decertification Office.

(3) The office and personnel support services of the Commission are carried out by the Directorate of the Professional Qualification and Certification Office.

(4) The term of office of the Student Representative, one of the members of the Commission, is one year, and that of the other members is three years. Members may be re-appointed at the end of this period.

(5) The duty of a member who does not attend the commission meeting twice within a calendar year without written permission and without an excuse, who is unable to perform his/her duty for more than six months due to illness, accident or any other reason, shall be terminated.

(6) If there is a vacancy in the memberships for any reason, the member who will replace the vacated member is determined by the Senate.

(7) The members of the Commission are shared with the public on the University's website.

Working Procedures and Principles of the Quality Commission

(1) The Quality Commission meets every six months. If necessary, the members of the Commission may be convened at the invitation of the Rector or at the written request of the absolute majority of the members of the Commission.

(2) The agenda items, place, date and time of the meeting are determined by the chairman; the commission members are notified in writing 2 (two) days in advance. The agenda of the meeting may be amended by the decision of the commission upon the proposal of the members before or during the meeting.

(3) The Quality Commission meets with an absolute majority of the total number of members and a decision is taken with an absolute majority. There is no abstention vote and if the votes are equal, the decision is made in accordance with the president's vote.

(4) Administrative and academic personnel of the University or expert persons may be invited to the meetings for technical support and consultancy purposes without the right to vote in the decisions.

(5) The secretariat services of the Quality Commission are carried out by the Directorate of the Professional Qualification and Certification Office. The decisions of the Commission are recorded in the Decision Book signed by the members of the Commission and a sequence number is given to the decisions from the beginning of each calendar year.

(6) The Quality Commission may form sub-working groups among its members if necessary. Dec.

(7) The Rector may assign academic and administrative personnel to the work of the Quality Commission if necessary.

(8) Administrative and academic unit quality commissions may be established with the proposal of the Quality Commission and the approval of the Senate decision in order to provide the necessary information and support to the Quality Commission. The unit quality commissions to be established represent the same unit; If the number of personnel working in the unit is one, they are assigned as the Unit Supervisor, if there is a number of personnel working more than one, they are assigned as the Unit Supervisor and the Unit Representative.

(9) University units are obliged to provide all kinds of information and documents requested by the commission within the scope of the Quality Assurance System.

Duties of the Quality Commission

(1) in line with the University's strategic plan and objectives within the framework of rules and principles as determined by the committee and the higher education quality education, research, and social contribution activities and administrative services assessment for the purpose of monitoring and improving the quality

a) To establish the internal and external quality assurance system of the University,

b) To identify the key performance indicators specific to the university,

c) To make a program evaluation,

d) To submit the studies within the scope of the above-mentioned works and transactions to the senate,

(2) To plan and carry out the internal evaluation studies of the University,

(3) Preparing the annual internal evaluation report of the institution containing the results of the institutional evaluation and improvement studies and submitting October to the Senate,

(4) that was approved by the Senate-year institution and the institution reports the internal evaluation internal evaluation internal evaluation of the institution attachments the attachments also of the report the scope of performance indicators, criteria and evidence, prepare and January of each year-March in the month created by the Higher Education Quality Council web-based system to install,

(5) To share the annual internal evaluation reports and October reports approved by the Senate with the public in an easily accessible way from the official website of the University,

(6) To make the necessary preparations for the corporate external evaluation program, to inform internal and external stakeholders about the process,

(7) To support the studies of the Higher Education Quality Board during the external evaluation process,

(8) the University's academic, research and administrative activities and social services contribution assessment, monitoring and implementation of internal and external quality assurance system in order to improve the quality, continuity and sustainability for the study of University insufficient funds in the budget request.

Formation of the Administrative and Academic Unit Quality Commission,

Working Procedures and Principles, Duties

Formation of the Administrative and Academic Unit Quality Commission

(1) Administrative and academic unit quality commissions are formed upon the proposal of the Quality Commission and the approval of the Senate decision.

(2) Academic unit quality commission members; It is formed by appointing two academic personnel, representing all faculties, institutes, colleges, vocational schools of the University, as the Head of the Unit and the Representative of the Unit. There is also a Student Representative among the members of the commission. Dec.

(3) Administrative unit quality commission members; Consists of all administrative units of the University by appointing administrative personnel as the Unit Supervisor if the number of personnel working in the unit is one, the Unit Supervisor if there are more than one employee, and the Unit Representative. There is also a Student Representative among the members of the commission. Dec.

(4) The office and personnel support services of the academic unit quality commission are carried out by the Faculty of Engineering and Architecture, the office and personnel support services of the administrative unit quality commission are carried out by the Industrial Cooperation and Technology Licensing Office Coordination

(5) The term of office of the Student Representative, one of the members of the Commission, is one year, and that of the other members is three years. Members may be re-appointed at the end of this period.

(6) The duty of a member who does not attend the commission meeting three times within a calendar year without written permission and without an excuse, who is unable to perform his/her duty for more than six months due to illness, accident or any other reason, shall be terminated.

(7) If there is a vacancy in the memberships for any reason, the member who will replace the vacated member is determined with the proposal of the Quality Commission and the approval of the Senate Decision.

(8) The members of the administrative and academic unit quality commissions are shared with the public on the University's website.

Working Procedures and Principles of the Administrative and Academic Unit Quality Commission

(1) The administrative and academic unit quality commission members hold separate meetings every four months, namely the administrative unit quality commission meeting and the academic unit quality commission meeting. If necessary, a meeting can also be held with the decision of the Quality Commission or the written request of the absolute majority of the quality commission members of the administrative and academic units.

(2) the academic unit Quality Commission's meeting agenda items, the place, date and time, unit Quality Commission administrative meeting agenda items and the Faculty of engineering and architecture, place, date and time, industry partnerships and Technology Licensing Office shall be determined by the coordinator; the members of the commission shall be notified in writing in advance of the days. The agenda of the meeting may be amended by the decision of the commission upon the proposal of the members before or during the meeting.

(3) Administrative and academic unit quality commissions are convened with an absolute majority of the total number of members and decisions are taken with an absolute majority. No abstentions are cast and in case the votes are equal, the voting round continues until an absolute majority is achieved.

(4) Administrative and academic personnel of the University or expert persons may be invited to the meetings for technical support and consultancy purposes without the right to vote in the decisions.

(5) The secretariat services of the academic unit quality commission are carried out by the Faculty of Engineering and Architecture, the secretariat services of the administrative unit quality commission are carried out by the Industrial Cooperation and Technology Licensing Office Coordination Office. The decisions of the Commission are recorded with the minutes of the meeting signed by the members of the Commission and a sequence number is given to the decisions from the beginning of each calendar year.

Duties of the Administrative and Academic Unit Quality Commission

(1) the University's strategic plan and the objectives and rules and principles as determined by the commission within the framework of quality education, research, and social contribution activities and administrative services assessment for the purpose of monitoring and improving the quality

a) To support the establishment of the internal and external quality assurance system of the University,

b) To support the identification of key performance indicators specific to the university,

c) To support the program evaluations,

d) To submit the works within the scope of the above-mentioned works and transactions to the Quality Commission,

(2) To support the planning and execution of the internal evaluation studies of the University,

(3) To provide support to the Quality Commission in the preparation of the annual internal evaluation report of the institution containing the results of the institutional evaluation and improvement studies,

(4) To support informing internal and external stakeholders about the process in making the necessary preparations for the corporate external evaluation program,

(5) To support the work of the Quality Commission during the external evaluation process of the Higher Education Quality Council.

THE FOURTH PART

Various and Final Provisions

Financial Structure of the Commission

(1) All expenses required for the services of the quality commission are covered from the University budget.

Cases Where There is No Provision

(1) In cases where there are no provisions in this directive, the Higher Education Quality Assurance and Higher Education Quality Board Regulation and other legislative provisions published in the Official Gazette dated November 23, 2018 and numbered 30604, as well as the Decisions of the University's Board of Directors and Senate, are applied.

Force

(1) This directive enters into force on the date of its adoption in the Senate.

Executive

(1) The Rector executes the provisions of this directive.

This content was updated on 05/07/2024.
